Party_Details.Name: Ng Heng Liat

Facts.Text: Ng Heng Liat is a director of Aquagen International Pte Ltd
Ng Heng Liat served as a director of Aquagen International Pte Ltd
Ng Heng Liat opposed AIPL's participation in arbitration proceedings
Ng Heng Liat received a notice from Kiyue Co Ltd under s 216A of the Companies Act

Party_Details.Issue: Whether directors can seek a declaration of compliance with management obligations without a pending action against them
Party_Details.Rule: Directors may seek a declaration of compliance with their duties if there is a reasonable apprehension of legal action
Party_Details.Application: They acted within their management obligations by opposing arbitration participation. the plaintiffs' actions were not in the company's best interest
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the plaintiffs were entitled to seek a declaration as there was no pending action against them.

Party_Details.Name: Kiyue Co Ltd

Facts.Text: Kiyue Co Ltd is a minority shareholder of Aquagen International Pte Ltd
Kiyue Co Ltd issued a notice under s 216A of the Companies Act to AIPL's directors
Kiyue Co Ltd requested AIPL to commence action against the plaintiffs for breaches of their fiduciary duties as directors of AIPL
Kiyue Co Ltd gave notice that it intended to apply to court under s 216A for leave to bring the action in the name and on behalf of AIPL if the directors did not comply

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a minority shareholder can independently initiate a counterclaim for directors' contravention of management obligations
Party_Details.Rule: A minority shareholder must obtain court approval to initiate a derivative action on behalf of the company
Party_Details.Application: It had the right to bring a counterclaim without court approval. only the company could seek redress for directors' actions
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court held that Kiyue Co Ltd lacked the standing to bring a counterclaim without court approval.

Party_Details.Name: Kiyue Co Ltd

Facts.Text: Kiyue Co Ltd is a minority shareholder of Aquagen International Pte Ltd
Kiyue Co Ltd issued a notice under s 216A of the Companies Act to AIPL's directors
Kiyue Co Ltd requested the directors of AIPL to commence action against the plaintiffs for breaches of fiduciary duties as directors to AIPL
Kiyue Co Ltd gave notice that, if the directors did not comply with its request, it intended to apply to court under s 216A for leave to bring the action in the name and on behalf of AIPL

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a minority shareholder can independently initiate a counterclaim for directors' contravention of management obligations
Party_Details.Rule: A minority shareholder must obtain court approval to initiate a derivative action on behalf of the company
Party_Details.Application: It had the right to bring a counterclaim without court approval. only the company could seek redress for directors' actions
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court held that Kiyue Co Ltd lacked the standing to bring a counterclaim without court approval.

Party_Details.Name: Ng Heng Liat

Facts.Text: Ng Heng Liat is a director of Aquagen International Pte Ltd
Ng Heng Liat served as a director of Aquagen International Pte Ltd
Ng Heng Liat was one of the plaintiffs who objected to AIPL's participation in the arbitration proceedings
Kiyue Co Ltd issued a notice on 29 July 2002 under s 216A of the Companies Act to the directors of AIPL, and Ng Heng Liat was one of those directors

Party_Details.Issue: Whether directors can seek a declaration of compliance with management obligations without a pending action against them
Party_Details.Rule: Directors may seek a declaration of compliance with their duties if there is a reasonable apprehension of legal action
Party_Details.Application: They acted within their management obligations by opposing arbitration participation. the plaintiffs' actions were not in the company's best interest
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the plaintiffs were entitled to seek a declaration as there was no pending action against them.

Party_Details.Name: Aquagen International Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: The entity is a private company limited by shares and the subject of a management dispute between its directors and a minority shareholder.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company can be joined as a defendant in a counterclaim brought by a shareholder who has not obtained court leave to represent the company's interests.
Party_Details.Rule: A company is a necessary party in any derivative proceeding, but the proceeding itself cannot move forward if the claimant fails to satisfy the statutory requirements for standing.
Party_Details.Application: The company was named as a defendant in the shareholder's counterclaim. The directors moved to strike out the claim on the basis that the shareholder lacked the authority to act for the entity.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court struck out the counterclaim against the company. It held that allowing the claim to proceed would bypass the protections of Section 216A, which are designed to prevent the company from being dragged into unauthorized litigation.
